education.notes: Merit Degree; Multiple commercial professional projects with industrial partners.
education.gpa_or_academic_standing: B+ (Quantitative GPA evaluation not provided by the college)

education.institution: Xi’an Jiaotong-Liverpool University & University of Liverpool
education.notes: Related Courses: Java/Python Programming (83%), Mobile Computing (85%), Game Design (81%), Human-Centric Computing (81%, Rank 1/83), Information Retrieval (91%).
education.gpa_or_academic_standing: First Class Honours; GPA: 77% (3.92/4.0 in US, 4.0/4.0 if excluding Foundation Year, Top 1 in 52); Awards: University Academic Achievement Awards 21/22; Best Overall Performance Award 22/23; University Academic Excellence Awards 22/23

work_experience.company: Shanghai NEW TOUCH Software Developing Company
responsibilities: Designed an internal management web for a local bank, focusing on usability and operational efficiency.
Proposed and implemented front-end solutions using HTML, CSS, and JavaScript.
Assisted in testing and validating SQL database integration and functionality.

projects.name: LLM-Powered Multi-Purpose Agent Applications on Discord
projects.description: Built LLM-embedded agent applications for specific tasks on Discord. Bot 1: role-playing bot enabling customizable, real-time character adaptation and emotional support using context engineering and dual-model coupling for dynamic persona control and sustained memory; integrated LoRA fine-tuning, async prompt distillation, and iterative injection to enhance performance while reducing API deposit cost by 20–30%. Bot 2: RAG-based Discord game assistant using Sentence-Transformer and ChromaDB to retrieve semantically relevant game lore and mechanics; performance enhanced via re-ranking and document augmentation.

projects.name: Digital Corpse Donator: Caring Digital Burial Process
projects.description: Conceptualized and designed an interactive experience where users curate, revise, and donate their posthumous data via digital interfaces to create social impact through personalized contributions; designed and led an activity-based workshop to conduct user research (n=41), ensuring ethical data handling and a usable interface through interviews, directed storytelling, cognitive walkthrough, and quantitative analysis.

projects.name: Lumen: A Light Management Platform
projects.description: Conducted market and user research on light intake and management, producing analytic reports to guide product development and marketing; designed and led pilot study and workshops, defining core concepts and design principles based on the Light-Time Impact model; created a Semi-Swiss-Cheese assessment model to estimate the risk of Seasonal Affective Disorder; explored innovative interfaces for human-light communication (light-audio spectrum transition; algae-oxygen based light translation).

projects.name: Facing Existential Crisis with LLM-Powered Agent
projects.description: Preliminary research on AI agents mediating human experiences of existential crisis under mentorship of Renwen Zhang (WKWSCI, Nanyang Technological University).

projects.name: Silent Reconnections: East Asian Perspectives on Managing Posthumous SNS Profiles
projects.description: Led semi-structured interviews (n=20) regarding cross-cultural approaches to digital remains management; coded and thematically analyzed qualitative data to identify emergent patterns in stewardship, memorialization, and identity continuity under mentorship of Ray LC (School of Creative Media, City University of Hong Kong).

projects.name: Data Visualization of IFSC Sport Climbing Competition and Athlete Data
projects.description: Developed D-IFSC, an interactive sport climbing data platform using HTML/CSS/JavaScript to improve accessibility and engagement; independently extracted, pre-processed, and analyzed athlete data (n=1683, p=15 max) using Python and Selenium; designed and iteratively optimized interactive domain-specific visualizations with Tableau and AmCharts JS; validated effectiveness via a user study (N=23), showing significant improvements over baseline in aesthetics (+1.47), usability (+1.14), and usefulness (+1.61), all p < 0.001.

projects.name: Distributed User Interface Framework for IoT Multi-Devices Environment (UnIEM)
projects.description: Developed UnIEM in Java, an interaction model for real-time reconfiguration of distributed user interfaces across dynamic IoT environments; configured core functionality with modular structure allowing adaptive UI distribution based on user preferences, accessibility, and context; developed a mobile app for video migration based on the framework and validated effectiveness via experiments simulating educational scenarios.

projects.name: OneClick
projects.description: Led the design and development of OneClick, an Android app inspired by iOS Shortcuts, allowing users to customize shortcuts for quick access to apps and actions; architected using OOP principles and implemented gesture recognition and floating windows; designed data structures for storing shortcuts, enabling lightweight, real-time shortcut management; used participatory design with questionnaires, interviews, and observation to enhance usability.

projects.name: TCP-based LEFT File Sharing Protocol Using Python
projects.description: Independently engineered a custom TCP-based file synchronization protocol in Python featuring optimized buffer management, breakpoint detection, and stateful session handling to maximize transfer throughput and ensure data integrity; scored 97/100 in stress-testing; structured core components with OOP (File Scanner, Listener, Downloader); developed a custom header system for file chunking and error detection, improving memory efficiency and preventing data loss or transfer loops.

projects.name: Torch: Smart Vibration-Feedback Guiding Glove for Visually-Impaired Group
projects.description: Identified pain points in assistive devices via interviews and contextual inquiry; engineered an infrared-based physical system converting distance data into nonlinear vibrotactile feedback; optimized haptic signal intuitiveness under complex conditions through user research; implemented the design using Arduino and developed an asynchronous monitoring system that adjusts sampling based on oscillation amplitude to handle perforated structures when guiding.

projects.name: Maple Valley (VR Game)
projects.description: Developed an immersive VR game emphasizing exploration, gathering, crafting, and building using Unity 3D and C++ scripting; designed and implemented a VR-adaptive inventory interaction system using position-based detection for intuitive, low-friction user control aligned with natural behaviors.

publications_or_talks: Aug 2023: Interactive Visualization of Sport Climbing Data. Authors: Fangze Qiu; Yue Li. Venue: INTERACT 2023 - IFIP Conference on Human-Computer Interaction (CCF Class C).
Dec 2024: Digital Corpse Donator: Caring Digital Burial Process. Authors: Fangze Qiu; Yue Li. Venue: Chinese CHI 2024.

Oct 2022: A Re-configurable Interaction Model in Distributed IoT Environment. Authors: Fangze Qiu; Huaxiao Huang; Yuji Dong. Venue: CyberC 2022 - International Conference on Cyber-Enabled Distributed Computing and Knowledge Discovery.

meta.confidence_notes: Contact details reconstructed confidently; name confirmed. UAL location inferred from institution name; other locations not explicitly stated. Some project technologies listed exactly as mentioned (e.g., Sentence-Transformer, ChromaDB, LoRA); platform/language assumptions avoided unless stated. Date 'Oct 2025 – Now' for research is taken verbatim though future start date is unusual; kept as provided. Unity 3D with C++ scripting is unusual but preserved from source text.
